pattern: Greedy single-swap: scan left-to-right and swap the first pair of adjacent same-parity digits that are out of order.
hints: Walk through adjacent pairs; two digits have the same parity when both are even or both are odd.
A swap makes the string smaller only when the left digit is strictly greater than the right digit.
Perform at most one such swap—take the first valid swap found from the left and stop.

edge_inputs.description: Already smallest, no beneficial swap exists
Two odd digits in decreasing order — swap them
Two even digits in decreasing order — swap them
Adjacent digits of different parity — no swap possible
First adjacent pair is same parity but already in order; second pair is beneficial
All same odd digit — no beneficial swap
Long string, beneficial swap at last position

stress.description: Random digit string of length up to 100
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
